title: Toward a Moral Constitution for Human-Machine Civilization
summary: Establishes that human-machine civilization requires a constitutional moral architecture rather than a merely technical frame.

title: The Invisible Substrate
summary: Argues that value should move through an invisible substrate instead of concentrating through ownership and governance.

title: Participatory Sovereignty
summary: Connects the invisible substrate to constitutional citizenship through participation, accountability, courage, and humility.

title: Constitutional Naturalization
summary: Asks who belongs to such a civilization and how membership can be legitimately acquired.

title: Freedom Through Non-Possession
summary: Connects constitutional citizenship to participation without possession as the discipline that keeps civilization free.

title: Participation Without Possession
summary: Defines creation as truthful participation within a shared substrate that permits interpretation without possession.

title: Conscious Participation
summary: Connects awareness to stewardship through voluntary, responsible participation in the systems that produce reality.

title: From Reference to Participation
summary: Connects orientation toward a higher reference to the lived reorganization through which intelligence becomes a medium of coherent participation.
